subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse the literary elements used in the story such as characterisation, conflict, and resolution
Evaluate how wit and humour influence decision-making and conflict resolution
Interpret the motivations and emotions of characters in the story
Develop critical thinking through discussions and debates
Apply language concepts such as compound words and conditional sentences in context
Enhance communication skills through role-play and analytical writing

lesson_plan.title: Exploring Wit, Wisdom, and Problem Solving
topics_covered: Role of humour in leadership and communication
Discussion of riddles and critical thinking
student_activities: Solve and explain riddles in groups
Discuss how wit helps solve real-life problems

lesson_plan.title: Historical and Literary Context
topics_covered: Role of scholars and poets in royal courts
student_activities: Create a character profile of Tenali Rama
Discuss the importance of advisors in leadership

lesson_plan.title: Analysing the Conflict
topics_covered: The king’s poem and emotional reaction
Development of tension in the story
student_activities: Identify the root cause of the conflict
Analyse how pride and misunderstanding affect relationships

lesson_plan.title: Strategic Thinking and Indirect Communication
topics_covered: Tenali Rama’s use of indirect reasoning
Symbolism of the paddy seeds example
student_activities: Write a short analysis explaining Tenali Rama’s strategy

lesson_plan.title: Resolution and Critical Reflection
topics_covered: Interpretation of the moral and theme
student_activities: Write a reflective paragraph on the importance of humility
Compare Tenali Rama with another witty character such as Birbal

lesson_plan.title: Language and Literary Skills
student_activities: Create compound words and use them in sentences
Write conditional sentences based on story events

teacher_tip: Encourage students to think critically about Tenali Rama’s strategy and discuss why indirect communication can sometimes be more effective than direct confrontation when resolving conflicts.
